name: Virginia
tagline: Home of the world's largest data-center market, a centralized IT-buying authority in VITA, and a first-in-the-nation agentic-AI pilot — Virginia is where AI infrastructure and government technology demand collide.

execSummary.summary: Virginia is the single most important state in the country for AI infrastructure: Loudoun County's 'Data Center Alley' is the densest concentration of data centers on the planet, and the hyperscale buildout shapes the state's politics, power grid, and tax base. For an SLG seller, three things matter most. First, that data-center boom is both an economic engine and a political fight — 15 data-center bills passed the 2026 General Assembly and a ~$1.6B/year sales-and-use tax exemption is the central dispute that has stalled the 2026–28 budget into June 2026. Second, government technology buying is unusually centralized: VITA (the Virginia IT Agency) sets policy, runs statewide contracts, and gates cloud through its COV Ramp security review. Third, leadership just turned over — Gov. Spanberger took office in January 2026 and named Mike Watson Commonwealth CIO in April 2026, with a stated focus on cloud transformation and AI adoption. Dell's strongest plays are AI-infrastructure / 'AI factory' demand and a centralized, governed buying motion through VITA.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure and 'AI factory' demand tied to the world's largest data-center market and the surrounding ecosystem (PowerEdge, networking, services).
Enterprise cloud transformation and AI adoption under a new Commonwealth CIO — modernization across 60-plus executive-branch agencies.
Governed, auditable AI to support the state's first-in-the-nation agentic-AI regulatory-review pilot and emerging AI-in-schools guidance.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh across a large centralized executive-branch IT footprint managed through VITA.

stats.label: New Commonwealth CIO
stats.note: Mike Watson appointed by Gov. Spanberger

budget.intro: Virginia budgets on a two-year (biennial) cycle running July 1 to June 30, with the General Assembly amending the off-year budget annually. After a months-long impasse (regular session ended March 14 without a deal; an April 23 special session failed), negotiators reached a ~$74B compromise on June 20, 2026, with floor votes scheduled June 23 and signing required before the July 1 deadline — the data-center sales/use tax exemption is retained but paired with a new $0.011/kWh data-center energy tax. As of June 22 the budget is agreed but not yet voted or signed. There is no single 'state IT budget' line — technology spend is distributed across agencies and run through VITA.

stats.label: 2026–28 introduced budget
stats.note: Youngkin two-year proposal; not yet enacted

breakdown.label: Data-center tax exemption (annual fiscal note)
breakdown.note: Per-year value at center of budget fight

notes: The 2026–28 budget was not enacted as of mid-June 2026 — confirm final appropriations before forecasting agency spend.
The data-center sales-and-use tax exemption (~$1.6B/yr) is the central political dispute and a proxy for the state's posture toward the AI-infrastructure boom.

FY2027 (outgoing Gov. Youngkin's proposed FY2027-28 biennial budget, per NASBO): $34.8B general fund, +6.2% vs FY2026; $99.96B all funds (FY2027). Structurally balanced with combined reserves at the 15% cap (~$4.3B).

initiatives.name: Data Center Alley & AI-Infrastructure Buildout
initiatives.summary: Loudoun County hosts the world's densest concentration of data centers, generating roughly 38% of the county's general-fund revenue. The 2026 General Assembly passed 15 of 61 data-center bills — adding siting/site-assessment requirements for 100MW+ facilities (HB 153), shifting grid-capacity costs to 25MW+ high-load users (HB 1393, HB 284), and water-reporting rules. The buildout is the strongest tailwind in the country for AI-infrastructure and ISG demand.

initiatives.name: Enterprise Cloud & AI Modernization (VITA)
initiatives.summary: Commonwealth CIO Mike Watson (appointed April 2026) leads enterprise IT strategy, cloud transformation, AI adoption, and digital modernization across the executive branch. With deep VITA and security tenure, his agenda points to governed cloud migration and agency AI enablement — an opening for private/hybrid infrastructure and services.

initiatives.name: Agentic-AI Regulatory Review Pilot (EO 51)
initiatives.summary: Executive Order 51 (July 2025) made Virginia the first state to deploy an agentic-AI tool to scan regulations and guidance for redundancy and statutory conflicts; from end-2025, executive-branch agencies must fold AI into their periodic regulatory reviews. Most broader AI legislation was tabled to 2027, so the near-term motion is operational AI adoption rather than new mandates — a chance to lead with governed, auditable AI.

initiatives.name: COV Ramp Cloud Security Review
initiatives.summary: COV Ramp (formerly ECOS), run by VITA, evaluates the security programs of SaaS/PaaS suppliers before agencies may procure them; 400-plus cloud products are assessed and approvals are valid 12 months and must be renewed. Any Dell cloud/as-a-service offering touching Commonwealth data must clear COV Ramp and align to the SEC530 security standard — a gate to plan for early.

procurement.intro: The Virginia IT Agency (VITA) is the centralized technology authority for the executive branch — it sets IT policy and security standards, runs statewide contracts, and manages sourcing for more than 60 agencies and 65,000-plus users. Most state technology buys run through VITA statewide contracts and its multisupplier service model, with delegated agency procurements still subject to VITA oversight.

vehicles.name: VITA Statewide Contracts
vehicles.detail: Primary vehicle. Pre-competed IT goods/services contracts available to executive-branch agencies and, in many cases, local government and higher-ed. Confirm Dell agreements and authorized resellers (incl. Software VAR) are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: VITA Multisupplier Model
vehicles.detail: Service-tower delivery model for infrastructure, cloud, and managed services across the Commonwealth IT enterprise.

vehicles.name: COV Ramp
vehicles.detail: Not a contract, but a mandatory cloud-security review gate for any SaaS/PaaS touching Commonwealth data (12-month approvals).

entities.name: VITA
entities.role: Central IT policy, security, and procurement authority — leads the statewide technology motion.

entities.name: Department of Planning & Budget (DPB)
entities.role: Owns the budget process; final 2026–28 appropriations gate agency spend.

entities.name: University systems (UVA, Virginia Tech, George Mason)
entities.role: Large independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers near Data Center Alley.

entities.name: Local government (Loudoun, Fairfax, Prince William)
entities.role: Data-center-rich localities with strong revenue and their own technology buying.

procurement.competition: Virginia is a centralized, contract-driven market — incumbents hold VITA statewide agreements across compute, networking, and services, and the dense federal/contractor presence in Northern Virginia makes it a sophisticated buyer. Differentiation comes from COV Ramp-ready offerings, AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center ecosystem, and services that map to cloud/AI modernization rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Abigail Spanberger
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Sworn in Jan 17, 2026; backed the June 20 budget compromise resolving the data-center tax fight and the 2026–28 budget impasse.

powerStructure.name: Mike Watson
powerStructure.title: Commonwealth CIO & VITA Agency Head
powerStructure.note: Appointed April 2026; 17+ years at VITA and former state CISO/deputy CIO. Owns cloud transformation and AI adoption.

powerStructure.name: Department of Planning & Budget (DPB)
powerStructure.title: Budget development & execution
powerStructure.note: Runs the biennial budget process — track for final 2026–28 appropriations.

powerStructure.name: General Assembly money committees
powerStructure.title: Appropriations & data-center policy
powerStructure.note: Senate (Lucas) vs. House (Scott) split on the data-center tax exemption drove the 2026 budget impasse.

news.title: Negotiators reach ~$74B 2026–28 budget compromise; floor vote set June 23, ahead of July 1 deadline
news.impact: Data centers keep the sales/use tax exemption but face a new $0.011/kWh energy tax (capped $600M/yr, ~$1.2B over the biennium); ends ~3-month impasse but not yet voted/signed — confirm enactment before forecasting agency spend.

news.title: Gov. Spanberger names Mike Watson Commonwealth CIO and VITA head
news.impact: New tech leadership focused on cloud transformation and AI adoption; predecessor Bob Osmond departs for Delaware.

news.title: Special budget session ends without a deal
news.impact: Budget uncertainty extends; agency IT planning constrained until appropriations land.

news.title: General Assembly passes 15 data-center bills (of 61)
news.impact: New siting, energy-cost, and water rules reshape data-center growth — the core AI-infrastructure market.

news.title: Most standalone AI legislation tabled to 2027 session
news.impact: Near-term AI motion is operational adoption (e.g., EO 51 pilot) rather than new statutory mandates.

news.title: Outgoing Gov. Youngkin proposed a $34.8B GF FY2027-28 biennial budget
news.impact: $99.96B all funds (FY2027), +6.2% vs FY2026 GF; structurally balanced with reserves at the 15% statutory cap (~$4.3B).

news.title: EO 51 launches first-in-the-nation agentic-AI regulatory-review pilot
news.impact: Signals state appetite for governed, auditable AI across executive-branch agencies.

dellActions: Lead with AI infrastructure: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to the world's largest data-center market and the surrounding ecosystem, plus university research-computing buyers in Northern Virginia.
Engage Commonwealth CIO Mike Watson's modernization agenda early — position governed cloud transformation and agency AI adoption across the centralized VITA enterprise.
Clear the COV Ramp gate before pursuing cloud/as-a-service deals, and confirm alignment to the SEC530 security standard for any offering touching Commonwealth data.
Validate that Dell's VITA statewide-contract coverage and authorized resellers (including Software VAR) are current and correctly scoped for target agencies.
Time agency-spend forecasts to the final 2026–28 appropriations — track DPB and the General Assembly money committees given the budget impasse.
